_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
3/12 8 PM Update: Well, that was one wild day here in the midst of one of the biggest storm cycle we've seen this season! Thanks to the entire Crystal Community for your patience today, and a huge shoutout to our Patrol and Mountain Ops teams for working to get the mountain open safely, as well as WSDOT for their work on 410 today. We have more snow on the way tonight through Friday.  While we are expecting a more normal day of storm skiing operations tomorrow with these extreme winds to forecast subside a bit, we all know the unpredictability of mother nature. Please give extra time heading up to the mountain tomorrow. As Patrol works to open the upper mountain, we ask everyone to continue to obey closures and respect the ropes. On big storm days, it is recommended to ski and ride with a parter- keep them in sight, and have a plan. Winter ain't over folks. We'll see you for pow turns tomorrow!

*24 Hour and Season-to-date snow totals are now posted for today. 
 
Events: 
  • The Campbell Basin Rail Jam has moved to next Saturday March 14th!  From 11am-2pm throw down your cleanest lines, stomp your best tricks, and compete for some seriously sweet prizes from our stacked sponsors. Not competing? No stress — pull up, hype your crew, and catch all the action from Campbell Basin Lodge (elite viewing vibes included).
  •  This is the last weekend of night skiing coming up March 12th-15th! Take advantage of all the new snow on your schedule. Enjoy our Coca Cola Ride and Dine deal for just $75. Included is a night skiing ticket (1pm-7pm), dinner voucher and a Coke. 
Notes for today:
  1. $199 Spring 3-Day Passes are on sale now! Ski for just $67 a day, any day of the week, for the rest of the season!
  2. $62 Day tickets continue every Tuesday for the remainder of the season!
  3. Looking to save time and make the most of the day? Consider grabbing a Daily Reserve Upgrade! Available on weekends and holidays, this upgrade grants access to priority lift lines, getting you on the lift faster and allowing you to complete more laps. 
  4. Did you know it just takes 5 Days to be entered to win a 26/27 Ikon Pass? Track your days on the Ikon App and register for RIDE TO WIN
  5. Paid reservations can only be cancelled by emailing SP+ at  CrystalMtnParking@spplus.com with Reservation #, First Name, and Last Name. While you may not receive an immediate reply, the cancellation will be processed. The Crystal Mountain Contact Center cannot process these cancellations.
  6. In case of on-mountain emergency: Call 360-663-3064 or 911

Mountain Weather 3/12/2026 5:30pm 

Forecaster: Mike Haft  
  
Synopsis:   
A narrow Jet keeps the Pnw in it’s crosshairs with continued snowfall through the extended.  The E-W nature of this Jet is proving to be quite beneficial.  Snow levels remain low enough with moderate winds.  Things dry out and cool down going into the weekend. 
  
Thursday Night:    
Sky Cover: Snow 
Freezing Elevation: 4000’  
12hr Precip: 7-11” possible  
Wind Trends: Moderate to Strong WSW 
Friday:   
Sky Cover: Snow 
Freezing Elevation: 4000’ 
12hr Precip: 6-10" possible 
Wind Trends: Moderate to Strong WSW  
Friday Night:    
Sky Cover: Snow Snow 
Freezing Elevation: Dropping to 2000’  
12hr Precip: 3-6" possible 
Wind Trends: Moderate WSW  
  
Extended:   
High pressure builds offshore and ushers even cooler air going into the weekend.  Some lingering showers are still in the cards but nothing impactful.  Get it while its good, a warmup is coming our way. 

*Daily Summaries: 12hr period representing 4am-4pm or 4pm-4am data
*Freezing Elevation (ft): Approximate elevation above sea level where the air temperature reaches 32 degree F

Uphill Travel Update: See the above banner for the current status. Please visit guest services to get your 25/26 uphill travel card. This must be renewed free of charge for the season. Uphill travel may be prohibited at any time as mountain operations teams conduct avalanche mitigation. Refer to the "No Uphill Travel" lights around the base area and the Mountain Report page indicating the status of the designated route. These lights are located by the Gondola trail map, at the bottom of Chinook, at the bottom of Discovery, and at the top of the Quicksilver lift shack. For more information about uphill travel, please visit the uphill travel page. 

25/26 WINTER PROGRAMS, CLINICS, AND NECESSITIES

  • Ski and Ride School: Explore our programs and find the right fit for you.
  • AIARE Course & Certification: Ski the backcountry with expert knowledge and certification
